channel.title: @hyperdbg - Telegram group archive
channel.description: Public archive of HyperDbg Telegram messages.

item.title: @getting458 on 2026-04-05 10:58:25+00:00 (#11539)
item.description: Technical Summary for Experts ​Phenomenon: Installing an EPT Hook (Execute-Only) on this page results in extreme Ping-Ponging (millions of Exec and Read violation switches per second). ​Core Issue: When MOVZX and JMP [mem] instructions are executed in an Execute-Only view, they attempt to read the Byte Table and Jump Table located within the same 4KB page. This forces the CPU to constantly toggle between the "Execution View" and the "Read/Write View" via VM-Exits. ​Driver Bottleneck: The driver-level Instruction Emulator fails to successfully emulate these instructions—specifically the MOVZX with complex SIB addressing—making it impossible to complete the instruction without a permission-based view switch.

item.title: @getting458 on 2026-04-05 10:58:13+00:00 (#11538)
item.description:
; --- Logic Start --- ; Location: Base + 0x1FB22D ; Opcode: 0F B6 04 03 MOVZX EAX, BYTE PTR [EBX+EAX] ; [!] Conflict Point 1: ; The instruction resides on this page. Execution requires reading ; data from page offset 0x260. Under an X-Only view, this read ; operation immediately triggers an EPT Read Violation.
; Location: Base + 0x1FB234 ; Opcode: FF 24 85 58 B2 XX XX JMP DWORD PTR [Base + 0x1FB258 + EAX*4] ; [!] Conflict Point 2: ; This is an indirect jump. The Jump Table is located at page ; offset 0x258. Similarly, in an X-Only view, reading the jump ; table address triggers an EPT Read Violation.
; --- Your Hook Point --- ; Location: Base + 0x1FB323 (Within the same 4KB page) ; Opcode: EB 0C JMP Base + 0x1FB331

item.title: @getting458 on 2026-04-05 10:55:37+00:00 (#11537)
item.description: I've perfectly fixed the jmp instruction, but movzx keeps failing to get the physical address and causing an exception because it does movxz first and then jmp

item.title: @getting458 on 2026-04-05 10:53:54+00:00 (#11536)
item.description: But I've already added instruction recognition, and I'll manually modify the registers and then exit the exception, but I don't know why I keep getting errors when trying to get the physical address

item.title: @getting458 on 2026-04-05 10:53:19+00:00 (#11535)
item.description: Because it read the entire 4kb page incorrectly, which caused the page to keep crashing when trying to read itself

item.title: @getting458 on 2026-04-05 10:52:48+00:00 (#11534)
item.description: It's just the EPT causing the ping-pong effect

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-04-05 08:21:24+00:00 (#11533)
item.description: Could you explain more?

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-04-05 08:21:15+00:00 (#11532)
item.description: I'm not sure if I understand the question 🤔

item.title: @getting458 on 2026-04-04 20:37:21+00:00 (#11531)
item.description: Can't seem to get the correct physical address

item.title: @getting458 on 2026-04-04 20:37:10+00:00 (#11530)
item.description: I wrote my own command recognizer but it sucks - it keeps failing all the time.

item.title: @getting458 on 2026-04-04 20:36:29+00:00 (#11529)
item.description: @HughEverett

item.title: @getting458 on 2026-04-04 20:36:19+00:00 (#11528)
item.description: ept hook How to handle reading and navigating to addresses on the same page?

item.title: @getting458 on 2026-04-04 20:36:01+00:00 (#11527)
item.description: jmp dword ptr [eax*4+On the same page]

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-04-04 19:24:51+00:00 (#11526)
item.description: Well, I don’t really agree with this. Not having HyperDbg blocked or blacklisted by antivirus programs is a major advantage, and it generally makes it much more convenient for users to work with.

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-04-04 18:27:03+00:00 (#11525)
item.description: I think you shouldn't disable any feature due to antiviruses. They will always flags things. Trying to make such a project obey them just returns diminishing results

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-04-04 18:20:44+00:00 (#11524)
item.description: We disabled it due to antivirus detection for now. You can enable it and recompile it if you wish.

item.title: @mariocy1 on 2026-04-04 18:19:31+00:00 (#11523)
item.description: !hide is not worked

item.title: @honorary_bot on 2026-04-03 17:44:47+00:00 (#11520)
item.description: Keep in mind that those require manual initialization though. So some code has to be written before going straight to io ports. I.e. it won’t work out of the box.

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-04-03 17:28:13+00:00 (#11519)
item.description: [discord] <unrustled.jimmies> [reply]: someone posted this earlier, https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07SVV6DV6?ref=ppx_yo2ov_dt_b_fed_asin_title exposes an IO interface which can work with hyperdbg if your motherboard doesnt have a real port or even a header for one. you can just need to change the port from 3f8 to whichever one it resolves to and recompile hyperdbg.

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-04-03 17:10:40+00:00 (#11518)
item.description: [discord] <learath2> The debugee thinks it managed to connect, it even thinks that synchronizing modules worked, but the debugger never really attaches, guess it's that the driver part just can't communicate through this fake serial connection, so it just never connects up

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-04-03 17:08:11+00:00 (#11517)
item.description: [discord] <learath2> I guess restarting did fix it, maybe I accidentally woke it up from hibernation or something. Overall bizarre experience with the usb serial thing, it kinda sorta works but also doesn't work 😄

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-04-03 17:00:22+00:00 (#11516)
item.description: Well, it is weird, because once you load HyperDbg (if you restart your computer), it should remove the previous VMM driver and reload it, so it might be a bug. Could you provide more information?

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-04-03 16:57:11+00:00 (#11515)
item.description: [discord] <learath2> Another quick question, I had a bit of an issue with having to kill hyperdbg while it was connecting, now I can't start it again because it says "previous instance of service is not fully deleted". How can I fix that?

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-04-03 16:53:39+00:00 (#11514)
item.description: Well, a USB serial needs a driver to convert from usb to serial. HyperDbg right now only uses I/O instruction (IN/OUT) for serial communication, so it is not likely to work.

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-04-03 16:50:41+00:00 (#11513)
item.description: [discord] <learath2> On a scale of there is no way to it should be fine, how likely is hyperdbg to work with a com device provided by a usb serial interface on the debugee?

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-04-03 12:09:09+00:00 (#11512)
item.description: What do you mean by VMI mode? If you mean running HyperDbg in VMI mode over network, yes it is being supported for year.

item.title: @instw0 on 2026-04-03 12:02:02+00:00 (#11511)
item.description: So the vmi mode is now also not available?

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-04-03 11:43:25+00:00 (#11509)
item.description: [discord] <hydra_dragon_antivirus> yeah it's pretty hard to add

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-04-03 11:42:28+00:00 (#11508)
item.description: But as we had a discussion earlier (I believe Artem told us) that it seems some hypervisor functionalities are different or they don't have some functionalities like MTF (Monitor Trap Flag) which HyperDbg uses them a lot. So, maybe some of the functionalities need to be changed.

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-04-03 11:40:58+00:00 (#11507)
item.description: Of course the best way of implementing it is using the debugger codes of HyperDbg and only add the AMD hypervisor part on top it.

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-04-03 11:40:04+00:00 (#11506)
item.description: Yes, feel free to work on it or if you have any proposal of how you can do it, you can discuss it here.

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-04-03 11:33:43+00:00 (#11505)
item.description: [discord] <hydra_dragon_antivirus> edit: typo

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-04-03 11:06:32+00:00 (#11504)
item.description: [discord] <hydra_dragon_antivirus> I was realized there no AMD support which might I should work on

item.title: @ghvv188 on 2026-04-03 06:10:06+00:00 (#11503)
item.description: Hi

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-04-02 20:37:03+00:00 (#11500)
item.description: [discord] <hydra_dragon_antivirus> hello

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-04-01 23:41:27+00:00 (#11499)
item.description: It's the matter of implanting an Intel e1000 driver for it, we tried it several times, but still not fully going through it. It's on the todo list but no one actively works on it as far as I know.

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-04-01 23:38:44+00:00 (#11498)
item.description: Ah, sorry I missed this message. It's on the todo list, I'm not personally working on it but there are others developers currently working on it. In Discord there is channel for this, you could see conversations there.

item.title: @instw0 on 2026-04-01 07:18:03+00:00 (#11496)
item.description: Hi! There is support for remote debugging through Ethernet?)

item.title: @MissRose_bot on 2026-03-31 01:41:21+00:00 (#11494)
item.description: Reported ⚜️LEBAP⚜️ [7001057901] to admins.​​​​​​​​​​

item.title: @1670864595 on 2026-03-31 01:41:21+00:00 (#11493)
item.description: /report

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 19:49:16+00:00 (#11485)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> I see why this was done for windows first as there is a lot more reversing potential to gain out of it

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 19:47:52+00:00 (#11484)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> how far along is porting hyperdbg to linux?

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:45:50+00:00 (#11483)
item.description: Otherwise, the process is same for say, hyperjacking linux. Or writing a separation kernel

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:45:30+00:00 (#11482)
item.description: We hyperjack Windows so we need Windows specific apis to allocate memory and run on all other cores

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:45:12+00:00 (#11481)
item.description: It's same for everything

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 19:44:58+00:00 (#11480)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> wouldnt it be easier on linux?

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 19:44:53+00:00 (#11479)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> also I was wondering why this guide is on windows

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:43:49+00:00 (#11478)
item.description: Except the revision identifier

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:43:35+00:00 (#11477)
item.description: Implementation specific

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 19:43:32+00:00 (#11476)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> alr lol

item.title: @honorary_bot on 2026-03-27 19:43:25+00:00 (#11475)
item.description: Yes, exactly

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 19:43:15+00:00 (#11474)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> so just give a page to vmxon and its gods plan?

item.title: @honorary_bot on 2026-03-27 19:42:34+00:00 (#11473)
item.description: Treat that region as opaque

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 19:41:54+00:00 (#11472)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> what does this vmxon region contain? All I find is this in the intel manual: > Before executing VMXON, software should write the VMCS revision identifier (see Section 26.2) to the VMXON > region. (Specifically, it should write the 31-bit VMCS revision identifier to bits 30:0 of the first 4 bytes of the > VMXON region; bit 31 should be cleared to 0.)

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:34:31+00:00 (#11471)
item.description: Vmcs and vmxon region are different

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 19:34:19+00:00 (#11470)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> [reply]: the vmcs?

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:33:52+00:00 (#11469)
item.description: Yes, thats what it does

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:33:45+00:00 (#11468)
item.description: Vmxon is required for other instructions like vmresume, vmlaunch

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 19:33:40+00:00 (#11467)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> oh that makes sense with the BIOS setting to enable virtualization

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:33:32+00:00 (#11466)
item.description: VMX bit enables vt-x extension. Vmxon loads the vmxon region required by each logical core

item.title: @honorary_bot on 2026-03-27 19:33:16+00:00 (#11465)
item.description: CR4 is usually set up by the bios and locked, so you can't modify it in runtime

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:33:02+00:00 (#11464)
item.description: No. Only one time

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:32:56+00:00 (#11463)
item.description: It doesn't have to do anything with guest/host separation

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 19:32:55+00:00 (#11462)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> so you need to enable vt-x twice

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:32:40+00:00 (#11461)
item.description: It's to enable vt-x for the cpu

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 19:32:27+00:00 (#11460)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> whats up with that cr4 bit you have to set?

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:32:02+00:00 (#11459)
item.description: Yup

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 19:31:58+00:00 (#11458)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> so thats all vmxon does?

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:31:30+00:00 (#11457)
item.description: Vmx non root mode is the guest

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:31:25+00:00 (#11456)
item.description: Vmx root mode is hypervisor

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 19:31:21+00:00 (#11455)
item.description: Vmx mode means cpu is capable of vt-x

item.title: @honorary_bot on 2026-03-27 19:10:51+00:00 (#11454)
item.description: It only means that it may use vmx instructions.

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 19:06:09+00:00 (#11453)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> if all cores are in vmx mode doesnt that mean they should be running a vmm and not an os?

item.title: @honorary_bot on 2026-03-27 18:33:03+00:00 (#11452)
item.description: All of the cores are usually "vmxon"-ed via IPI

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 18:31:54+00:00 (#11451)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> so in a type2 hypervisor a certain number of cores are in VMXON mode at any given time and the others are reserved for the OS? is that what giving a VM a certain number of cores do?

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 17:56:20+00:00 (#11450)
item.description: So if a core is hypervised, the vmx mode must be on. You might be confusing vmx root and non root operating modes

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 17:54:51+00:00 (#11449)
item.description: Vmx mode has to be enabled for any vt-x instruction to be recognized

item.title: @honorary_bot on 2026-03-27 17:32:40+00:00 (#11448)
item.description: Yes, vmxon affects only the current CPU thread. It's totally possible to enable VMX only for a subset of the cores. What problems do you see in that scenario?

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 17:29:12+00:00 (#11447)
item.description: [discord] <fexsped> I was reading [here](https://rayanfam.com/topics/hypervisor-from-scratch-part-2/) about how VMX needs to be enabled through cr4 and then having to switch to vmx operating mode using VMXON. Does this VMXON take effect only on the current core? how do type2 hypervisors work while also allowing the underlying system to function without beint in VMX operating mode?

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 11:56:25+00:00 (#11446)
item.description: Yep it does

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-03-27 11:54:58+00:00 (#11445)
item.description: I think that, by default, if we allocate 4096 bytes, Windows gives us an aligned memory address (at least from what I vaguely remember).

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 11:45:31+00:00 (#11444)
item.description: Ask the author of code (Sina I guess), my guess is that he didn't know it returned a page aligned address

item.title: @CheapNow on 2026-03-27 11:09:24+00:00 (#11443)
item.description: 2* PAGE_SIZE when ExAllocatePool2 already hands back an aligned address is retarded

item.title: @CheapNow on 2026-03-27 11:09:02+00:00 (#11442)
item.description: I was waiting for your justification

item.title: @asiker123 on 2026-03-27 10:40:26+00:00 (#11441)
item.description: thankyou

item.title: @HughEverett on 2026-03-27 10:38:54+00:00 (#11440)
item.description: If you are using HyperDbg in the debugger mode, you don't need to install the driver or anything on your physical machine. You just need to install drivers in the guest while HyperDbg on the side of the host acts as a (user-mode) serial communication app.

item.title: @asiker123 on 2026-03-27 10:35:25+00:00 (#11439)
item.description: I’ve already installed the HyperDbg VMM driver on my physical machine. Do I still need to install the VMM driver on the Windows 10 virtual machine in VMware?

item.title: @HyperDbgBot on 2026-03-27 06:31:52+00:00 (#11437)
item.description: [discord] <livanec_smaslem> [reply]: in hyperdbg source it is 3. they also add ALIGNMENT_PAGE_SIZE which is another 4096. i dont understand the reasoning https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/962350355839066130/1486976028139192431/image.png?ex=69c775d7&is=69c62457&hm=7ab6484185598200a6d4c252193726dc3be56ceca49a17b30f388cccfe690a2b&

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 04:03:48+00:00 (#11436)
item.description: No we do in part 3's source. It wasn't 3 though. int VMXONSize = 2 * VMXON_SIZE;

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 04:00:59+00:00 (#11435)
item.description:
#define ALIGNMENT_PAGE_SIZE 4096
Yeah. There was no such allocation

item.title: @staarblitz on 2026-03-27 03:56:21+00:00 (#11434)
item.description: Then what it is for

item.title: @CheapNow on 2026-03-27 03:49:50+00:00 (#11433)
item.description: 3 pages to align a page from an allocator that already returns page aligned memory.. whoever the fuck taught you this is brain damaged and needs to be retroactively aborted
